notes: Static threat-intel snapshot bundled with D.4 v0.1. Phase 1c replaces this with live feed integration via D.8 Threat Intel Agent (VirusTotal + OTX + CISA KEV + abuse.ch).
sources: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog — IPs extracted from advisories
abuse.ch — Feodo Tracker (botnet C2 IP list)
MITRE ATT&CK Group/Software references — known threat-actor infrastructure

tag_legend.tor_exit: IP belongs to a published Tor exit-node range; not inherently malicious, but inbound from one is a strong signal for further review.
tag_legend.known_bad: IP / domain has appeared in at least one of: CISA KEV, abuse.ch Feodo Tracker, MITRE ATT&CK Group infrastructure list.
tag_legend.dynamic_dns: Domain uses a public dynamic-DNS provider — common in C2 channel hosting (attackers use them to rotate infrastructure quickly).
tag_legend.url_shortener: Shortened URL — usually benign, but a leading indicator for phishing campaigns.
tag_legend.tunnel: Public tunnel/relay service — operators rarely whitelist these; their appearance in egress traffic is a strong signal.
